title: Benchmark Measurement Record
description: One measurement of ONE story under ONE arm of the pipeline-vs-raw-prompting benchmark (IMPROVEMENT-PLAN Phase 5, docs/benchmark-protocol.md). Records are appended as JSONL to evidence/benchmark.jsonl by scripts/benchmark-capture.js; each LINE validates against this schema. Arm A = raw prompting; Arm B = the pipeline via the runner. Metrics are defined in the protocol doc; absent metrics are recorded as null (not omitted) so a gap is explicit, never silently a zero.

story_id.description: The benchmarked story. Matches the id used in docs/benchmark-protocol.md's selection table; not necessarily a live context.json story.

arm.description: Which arm produced this measurement. 'raw' = a single model prompted directly ('write Playwright tests for this story') with reasonable follow-ups, timeboxed. 'pipeline' = the full gated pipeline via npm run pipeline.

recorded_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this measurement was recorded.

model.description: Optional. The model id used for this arm, so a later reader can attribute results to a model version.

operator.description: Who ran this arm (the human doing the benchmark). Null if not recorded.

track.description: Optional. For the pipeline arm, the track the story ran on (lite/standard/full); lets results be sliced by ceremony level.

metrics.description: The measured outcomes. Every field is nullable: null means 'not measured / not applicable for this arm', which is an HONEST gap — never coerce it to 0. Definitions and formulas live in docs/benchmark-protocol.md.

time_to_first_green_test_min.description: Wall-clock minutes from starting the arm to the first test that runs green against the app. Lower is better.

gate4_corrections.description: Pipeline arm: count of Gate-4 corrections before the code was acceptable. Raw arm: corrections-to-acceptable judged against the SAME §4 Gate-4 checklist. Lower is better.

fictional_test_rate.description: Fraction (0..1) of assertions about behavior never observed in the running app — the rule-8 signal. Lower is better; the pipeline's no-tests-from-text-alone rule should drive this toward 0.

selector_survival_rate.description: Fraction (0..1) of locators that still resolve after replay against >=2 later app versions (scripts/selector-survival.js). Higher is better. Null when app history is unavailable — record the justification in docs/evidence.md, do not fake a number.

known_bug_catch_rate.description: Fraction (0..1) of the story's known post-ship bugs that this arm's tests would have caught. Higher is better.

traceability_coverage.description: Fraction (0..1) of produced tests that carry a resolvable STORY->RISK->TC->test link. Expected ~0 for the raw arm (it produces no traceability chain) — say so rather than treating it as a defect of the measurement.

notes.description: Free text: where this arm struggled or surprised, anything the numbers don't capture. Feeds docs/evidence.md, including where raw prompting WON.
